title: eriogonum jamesii subsp. bakeri
intro: Eriogonum jamesii subsp. bakeri, also known as Baker's buckwheat, is a subspecies of the James' buckwheat plant, a flowering shrub native to the southwestern United States and northern Mexico. The plant is rich in medicinal properties, particularly its roots, which contain a compound called glycosides that have anti-inflammatory and antimicrobial effects. The health benefits of Baker's buckwheat include its ability to soothe digestive issues, reduce inflammation, and support immune function, making it a valuable herb in traditional medicine. Baker's buckwheat can be prepared as a tea, infusion, or tincture, and is often combined with other herbs to enhance its medicinal properties.
